description: This image displays a collection of 13 low-resolution, false-colour satellite or aerial imagery snippets, arranged in three rows (five, five, and three tiles respectively). Each square or rectangular tile depicts a different patch of land, showcasing varied land cover/land use (LULC) patterns. The dominant features across the snippets are agricultural fields, characterised by distinct rectangular and irregular parcels rendered in a palette of reds, oranges, purples, light blues, and greens. Some tiles also show features that resemble impervious surfaces or built-up areas, appearing as white or light grey rectangular patches, particularly in the first row, fourth column snippet. One tile in the second row, first column, is almost entirely uniform green, possibly representing a forest or extensive grassland, with a very faint, unreadable pattern in its center. The images demonstrate a mosaic of landscapes, including rural agricultural areas and potentially some fragmented urban or infrastructure elements. No specific text, labels, or temporal information is visible.
